title: andropogon nardus var. marginatus
intro: Andropogon nardus var. marginatus, also known as Kans grass or sweet cane, is a variety of the Andropogon nardus plant, a type of perennial grass native to Australia and the Pacific Islands. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of fever. The health benefits of Andropogon nardus var. marginatus include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating various skin conditions, such as burns and cuts. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
